events.title: US Resumes Blockade on Iranian Ports Amid Renewed Hostilities
events.desc: Less than a month after signing a memorandum of understanding, the United States has reinstated a naval blockade on Iranian ports. This action escalates tensions following a period of skirmishes and mutual claims of violations, signaling a significant breakdown in the recent diplomatic efforts.

events.title: US Attacks Iran, IRGC Claims Strikes on US Military Sites
events.desc: The United States has launched a wave of air strikes against Iran, coinciding with claims by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) of striking US military sites in the Gulf. The renewed hostilities have led to a spike in oil prices and underscore the fragility of the recent maritime security agreements.

events.title: US Military Conducting Further Strikes Against Iran
events.desc: The U.S. military has announced it is conducting additional strikes against Iran, commencing Tuesday afternoon Eastern Time. These actions follow the U.S. reinstatement of a blockade on Iranian shipping and Iran's closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Iran Threatens Further Seaway Closures Amidst US Blockade
events.desc: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) has threatened to close "all other export corridors that benefit the U.S. and its allies" following the U.S. reimposition of a naval blockade on Iranian ports. Iran had previously closed the Strait of Hormuz, escalating regional tensions.

events.title: Iran-Linked Vessels Transit Hormuz Before US Blockade Takes Effect
events.desc: The number of vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz increased on Tuesday, with a majority linked to Iranian trade, shortly before the U.S. blockade went into effect on Wednesday. U.S. President Trump had previously reimposed the blockade and threatened further action if negotiations did not resume.

events.title: US Air Strikes Target Iran as Tensions Escalate
events.desc: The United States has conducted a third consecutive night of air strikes against Iran. The strikes occurred as President Trump announced a new charge on shipping and a blockade on the Strait of Hormuz, further intensifying the conflict.

events.title: US and Iran Exchange Fire Amid Rising Tensions Over Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The United States and Iran have engaged in retaliatory attacks, with Iran claiming to have struck US military assets in Kuwait, Jordan, Bahrain, and Oman shortly after US strikes. The conflict has intensified around the critical Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: US Insists Strait of Hormuz Open Amid Strikes with Iran
events.desc: Despite Iran's claims of closing the Strait of Hormuz and launching wide-ranging attacks, the US maintains that the vital waterway remains open. The exchange of strikes continues as the conflict deepens.

events.title: US and Iran Trade Attacks as Khamenei is Buried
events.desc: US Central Command reports hitting 90 Iranian targets in the latest round of strikes. The Iranian health ministry stated that 14 people have been killed since Tuesday, amidst the ongoing exchange of fire and the funeral of Ayatollah Khamenei.

events.title: Iran's Nuclear and Military Damage Revealed in Released Satellite Images
events.desc: Restricted satellite imagery has revealed damage to Iranian targets, including ballistic missile infrastructure, nuclear sites, and naval bases, following recent strikes.

events.title: Israeli Strike on Gaza Police Post Kills Seven
events.desc: An Israeli military strike on a police post in northern Gaza has resulted in the deaths of seven people, including a senior officer in the Hamas-run police force. The Israeli military stated that the targets were "terrorists."

events.title: Houthi Missiles Target Saudi Arabia After Strikes on Sanaa Airport
events.desc: The Iran-backed Houthi group has launched missiles at Saudi Arabia, targeting Abha airport. The Houthis claim this is in retaliation for an attack on Sanaa airport, blaming Saudi Arabia for the strike in Yemen's capital.

events.title: G7 Urges Halt to Attacks in Sudan's El-Obeid, Calls for Arms Embargo
events.desc: G7 foreign ministers and the EU's foreign policy chief have called for an immediate cessation of hostilities by Sudan's Rapid Support Forces and allied groups in El-Obeid. They also urged all parties to allow aid access and engage in peace talks, while advocating for a broader arms embargo.

events.title: US Expands Sanctions on Iran's Oil and Cryptocurrency Sectors
events.desc: The United States has broadened its sanctions targeting Iran, focusing on its oil sector and cryptocurrency activities. The Treasury Department stated that the targeted network, linked to shipping magnate Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani, remains a key player in Iran's oil exports and has expanded into global commodities trading.

events.title: AI Threat Fusion Center Proposed Amidst Concerns Over AI Model Safety
events.desc: Following concerns over the cyber potential of AI models like Anthropic's Mythos 5, and government intervention to restrict its release, there is a growing call for an AI Threat Fusion Center. This center would aim to provide cyber defenders with controlled access to AI models and standardize processes for managing AI risks.
